title: various different test fixes
body:
see commits, various test fixes for different issues and flakes.

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [ ] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [ ] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [ ] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [ ] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@timcoding1988 @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

I think it would make sense to pick this into 5.7 as well as it should help reducing flakes to make the release process faster.
I wonder if adding `--network=none` to most of our `podman` and `buildah` invocations isn't a good idea in general - speed the tests up and reduce potential flakes?

> I wonder if adding --network=none to most of our podman and buildah invocations isn't a good idea in general - speed the tests up and reduce potential flakes?

Speed up sure, but this is not what most people use so sticking to the default makes sense and has in the past shown several network setup issues. The issue with this test is that we kill buildah so the interfaces ,etc... are all leaked because there is no cleanup process like podman has.

@lsm5: new pull request created: #27492

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27472#issuecomment-3512296231):

>/cherrypick v5.7


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes-sigs/prow](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/prow/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
